narrative: FY2023-24 shows a more balanced but bifurcated benchmark. JSW, SAIL, and Jindal Stainless all report 100% coverage for both product environmental/social information and recycling/safe-disposal information, while Tata remains materially below the leaders at 53% and 16%. However, Tata leads the openness-of-business channel indicators, with 1,176 dealers/distributors and zero trading houses, indicating broad sales reach and no trading-house purchase dependence under the reported definition. Jindal reaches 100% product-information coverage and 240 dealers/distributors, but its procurement model shows high intermediary exposure, with 500 trading houses and 78% purchases from trading houses. SAIL combines 100% product-information coverage with zero trading-house purchases, but has a smaller dealer/distributor base at 194. Therefore, product-responsibility disclosure leaders are JSW, SAIL, and Jindal, while Tata leads market-channel breadth and procurement-route simplicity. The year also marks Tata’s transition into more comparable disclosure after restatements and boundary changes.
